full_text_rationale: Full text describes Online Repository Memory that learns reusable skills from chronological historical commits and evaluates future pull-request generation on held-out later repository changes.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 010; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Mo Li, L. H. Xu, Qitai Tan, Ting Cao, Yunxin Liu
titulo: Learning to Commit: Generating Organic Pull Requests via Online Repository Memory
formato_armazenamento: Online Repository Memory represented as a continuously updated repository-specific skill document derived from chronological commits through blind attempt, oracle diff comparison, contrastive reflection, and CRUD skill updates.
framework_agente: Learning to Commit repository-adaptive coding agent with supervised contrastive reflection on historical commits
llm_utilizado: Claude Opus 4.6; Claude Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1 Pro used as judges
metodo_avaliacao: chronological held-out future-task evaluation; deterministic code metrics; pairwise LLM judging; case study
benchmarks_utilizados: Internal expert-maintained reinforcement-learning training repository with 2,738 non-merge commits filtered to 386 substantive commits; 24 historical learning commits and 7 future held-out test tasks
metricas_desempenho: File IoU, trajectory steps, line deviation ratio, pairwise win rate, Q1 scope alignment, Q2 logic similarity, Q3 redundancy and hallucination, Q4 code style
metricas_cl: Skill-document updates through on-policy contrastive reflection; chronological temporal split; sequential versus parallel learning modes; all-corpus versus category-specific curricula
baseline_comparado: Base agent without skill document under the same Claude Opus 4.6 tool-use capabilities
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Pilot starts from 2,738 non-merge commits, filters to 386 substantive commits, and uses 24 historical learning commits to build memory before 7 future test tasks; diffs over 180K tokens are excluded.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; paper reports trajectory steps but not monetary or token cost.
resultado_principal: Skill-conditioned agents improve file localization, reduce patch bloat, and win more pairwise LLM-judge comparisons than the no-skill baseline in three of four settings under both judges.
desempenho_vs_baseline: Table 2 reports File IoU improvements in three of four settings, including 80% versus 61% in seq-all and 80% versus 68% in par-bycat. Table 3 reports skill-agent win rates of 54%/57% in par-bycat and 55%/58% in seq-all under Claude/Gemini judges.
custo_vs_baseline: The skill-conditioned agent uses fewer trajectory steps in seq-all (56.8 versus 71.9) and slightly fewer or similar steps in most settings, but no monetary cost is reported.
limitacoes: Evaluation is a pilot on one highly coupled expert-maintained repository with 7 future held-out tasks; LLM judges may introduce bias; public multi-repository generalization is left for future work.
ameacas_validade: Synthetic issue generation, LLM-based commit filtering and judging, small future-test sample, single repository domain, temporal split construction, and skill verbosity may influence the organicity results.
